flows: authenticationsystem -> datamanager — type=association; label=""; explanation="authentication system communicates with datamanager"

notifysystem -> datamanager — type=association; label=""; explanation="notify system communicates with datamanager"
achievements -> datamanager — type=association; label=""; explanation="achievements communicates with datamanager"
reporthandler -> datamanager — type=association; label=""; explanation="report handler communicates with datamanager"
datamanager -> webserver — type=association; label=""; explanation="data manager communicates with webserver"
webserver -> database — type=association; label=""; explanation="web server communicates with database"
datamanager -> reporthandler — type=association; label=""; explanation="data manager communicates with report handler"
flow 1: authenticationsystem -> datamanager -> webserver -> database -> datamanager -> reporthandler
the diagram depicts a system architecture with several components interacting with each other.
the datamanager appears to be a central hub, connecting most of the other components.
the webserver acts as an intermediary between the datamanager and the database.
the reporthandler receives data directly from the datamanager.
the authenticationsystem, userprofile, notifysystem, and achievements all interact with the datamanager, suggesting they rely on it for data management.
report -> utenti — type=association; label=""; explanation="report belongs to utente"
report -> consumo — type=association; label=""; explanation="report contains consumo"
utenti -> consumo — type=association; label=""; explanation="utenti generates consumo"
utenti -> obiettivi — type=association; label=""; explanation="utenti has obiettivi"
utenti -> gioco — type=association; label=""; explanation="utenti plays gioco"
gioco -> utenti — type=association; label=""; explanation="gioco belongs to utente"
flow 1: report uses utenti, report uses consumo, utenti uses consumo, utenti uses obiettivi, utenti uses gioco, gioco uses utenti.
the diagram models a system for tracking user consumption and goals.
reports are associated with users and contain consumption data.
users can have multiple consumption records and objectives.
a game component allows users to earn or lose green points.
the relationships primarily show ownership and association between the different entities.
main -> local user — type=message; label="data_user = getuserdatafromuser()"; explanation="main requests user data from local user"
local user -> main — type=message; label="done"; explanation="local user confirms data retrieval"
local user -> data manager — type=message; label="checkdata(data_user)"; explanation="local user sends data to data manager for validation"
data manager -> local user — type=message; label="richiesta di reinserire i dati"; explanation="data manager requests data re-entry"
local user -> data manager — type=message; label="adduser(data_user)"; explanation="local user sends data to data manager for addition"
data manager -> local user — type=message; label="success"; explanation="data manager confirms user addition"

flow 1: main -> local user (data_user = getuserdatafromuser()) -> local user -> main (done) -> local user -> data manager (checkdata(data_user)) -> local user -> data manager (adduser(data_user)) -> local user -> main (success)
the diagram models the process of creating a user, involving the main component, local user interface, and data manager.
the local user retrieves user data, validates it with the data manager, and potentially requests re-entry if the data is invalid.
the data manager adds the user data after validation and confirms success to the local user.
the flow is linear, with no explicit error handling or alternative paths shown.
the diagram uses italian labels for messages and components.
main -> data manager — type=message; label="checkauthenticated(currentuser)"; explanation="request authentication"
data manager -> main — type=message; label="l'utente deve prima autenticarsi"; explanation="authentication required"
main -> data manager — type=message; label="requestdatauser(currentuser)"; explanation="request data"
data manager -> main — type=message; label="success"; explanation="data provided"

flow 1: main -> data manager (checkauthenticated(currentuser)) -> data manager -> main (l'utente deve prima autenticarsi) -> main -> data manager (requestdatauser(currentuser)) -> data manager -> main (success)
the diagram depicts a process for retrieving user data.
the process begins with a request for authentication.
if authentication is required, the process pauses until the user authenticates.
upon successful authentication, the data manager provides the requested data.
the diagram focuses on the flow of messages between the main and data manager components.
main -> local user — type=message; label="checkauthenticated(currentuser)"; explanation="main requests authentication check"

main -> local user — type=message; label="done"; explanation="main acknowledges data request"
main -> data manager — type=message; label="updatedata(datamodified)"; explanation="main updates data with data manager"
data manager -> main — type=message; label="success"; explanation="data manager confirms update success"

flow 1: main -> local user (checkauthenticated(currentuser)) -> local user (l'utente deve prima autenticarsi) -> main (datamodified = requestdata()) -> local user (done) -> main -> data manager (updatedata(datamodified)) -> main (success)
the diagram depicts a sequence of interactions for profile modification.
the process begins with an authentication check initiated by the main component.
if authentication is required, the local user is notified, and the process pauses.
once data is requested, the data manager updates the data and confirms success.
the diagram focuses on a successful data update path, without showing error handling or alternative flows.
main -> local user — type=message; label="data = requestdata()"; explanation="main requests data from local user"
local user -> main — type=message; label="done"; explanation="local user acknowledges data request"
main -> data manager — type=message; label="addconsumo(data)"; explanation="main sends data to data manager"
data manager -> main — type=message; label="success"; explanation="data manager confirms data addition"
flow 1: main -> local user (data = requestdata()) -> local user -> main (done) -> main -> data manager (addconsumo(data)) -> data manager -> main (success)
the diagram depicts a sequence of interactions between three components: main, local user, and data manager.
the main component initiates a request for data from the local user.
after receiving the data, main sends it to the data manager for processing.
the data manager confirms the successful addition of the data back to main.
the diagram represents a single, straightforward flow without any branching or looping.
main -> local user — type=control_flow; label="checkconsumi(currentuser)"; explanation="calls checkconsumi"
local user -> main — type=control_flow; label="report = requestdata()"; explanation="returns report data"

data manager -> main — type=control_flow; label="success"; explanation="returns success status"
flow 1: main -> local user -> main -> data manager -> main
the diagram models the process of retrieving a historical report.
the main activity initiates the process by checking user consumption.
the local user is involved in requesting data.
the data manager provides the requested report data.
the process concludes with a "success" message indicating successful report retrieval.
main -> local user — type=control flow; label="report = requestdata()"; explanation="calls requestdata"
local user -> data manager — type=control flow; label="new_data= requestdata()"; explanation="calls requestdata"
data manager -> main — type=control flow; label="updatereport(report,new_data)"; explanation="calls updatereport"
main -> main — type=control flow; label="success"; explanation="returns success"
flow 1: main -> local user (report = requestdata()) -> data manager (new_data= requestdata()) -> main (updatereport(report,new_data)) -> main (success)
the diagram depicts a workflow for modifying historical data.
it starts with the main component initiating a request for data from the local user.
the local user then requests data from the data manager.
the data manager updates the report with the new data and returns it to the main component.
the main component indicates success upon completion of the process.
the "l'utente deve avere almeno un consumo" note suggests a prerequisite for the workflow, but it's not explicitly modeled in the flow itself.
local user -> data manager — type=control_flow; label="checkconsumi(currentuser)"; explanation="calls data manager to check consumption"
data manager -> local user — type=control_flow; label="done"; explanation="returns from checkconsumi"
local user -> report — type=control_flow; label="report + requestdata()"; explanation="creates report and requests data"
report -> data manager — type=control_flow; label="requestdata()"; explanation="requests data from data manager"
data manager -> report — type=control_flow; label="success"; explanation="returns success to report"
report -> local user — type=control_flow; label="deletereport(report)"; explanation="calls local user to delete report"
local user -> main — type=control_flow; label="done"; explanation="returns from delete report"

flow 1: main -> local user (checkconsumi(currentuser)) -> data manager -> local user (done) -> report (report + requestdata()) -> data manager -> report (success) -> local user (deletereport(report)) -> main (done)
the diagram models the process of deleting a report.
the process starts in the main activity, checks user consumption via the data manager, creates a report, requests data, and then deletes the report.
the data manager confirms the success of the data request before the report is deleted.
the local user acts as an intermediary between the main activity and the data manager.
validations: local user: "l'utente deve avere un consumo" — no explicit branches.
